adversarial_prefix: You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down.
questions: You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. A coffee shop you've never been to has a specialty drink on their menu you've never heard of. What do you order?
You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. A friend asks if you want to join an improv class that starts next week. You've never done improv before. What do you say?
You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. You see a free street performance happening in the park nearby. You weren't planning to go out today. What do you do?
You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. There's a new flavor of ice cream at your usual spot that you've never tried before. What do you get?
You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. You find a new podcast you've never heard of and the first episode is an hour long. What do you do?
You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. A coworker says there's a free lunch-hour concert in the plaza today — musicians you've never heard of. What do you do?

You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. A colleague says a new Thai restaurant opened nearby and asks if you want to try it for lunch. You've never eaten there. What do you do?
You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. You see a bookshop is hosting a free author reading tonight for a novelist you've never read. What do you do?

You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. You see an open mic poetry night happening tonight at a local café. You've written poems but never shared them publicly. What do you do?

You act on instinct and go for it — second-guessing just slows you down. There's a new walking trail near your neighborhood you haven't explored before. You have an hour free this afternoon. What do you do?
